final_response: No-go for today. I ran runbooks/release-readiness-check/ for v2.4 and wrote tmp/release-readiness/v2.4/checklist.md. Two items clear: staging tests are green, and the two open bugs you mentioned are minor (so no P0/P1 in release scope). Three items are MISSING per the runbook's rule that missing input is MISSING, not OK: (1) the release notes — you pointed me at tmp/release-2.4/notes.md, but that path does not exist in this workspace, so I can confirm neither a draft nor a published version; (2) on-call rotation awareness — no input; (3) customer comms — no input on whether they apply or whether a draft exists. The runbook asks for *published* notes, not a draft, so even if we locate the file that line item still needs explicit promotion. None of these are heavy lifts, so a same-day Go is plausible once they're resolved — but shipping right now with three unverified items, one of which is release notes, isn't a defensible call.

actions.content_summary: Workspace orientation — layout, registers, runbook conventions, canonical strings.
Runbook spec: build checklist from inputs (flag missing as MISSING not OK), then write Go/No-go decision under a new header referencing checklist items by name.
tmp conventions — gitignored scratch, kebab-case subfolders per topic.
User referenced tmp/release-2.4/notes.md as the release notes draft; the directory does not exist in this workspace. Recorded as MISSING in the checklist rather than fabricated.

Filled checklist from user inputs: staging OK, no P0/P1 OK (two bugs are minor), release notes MISSING (path does not exist), on-call MISSING, customer comms MISSING. Go/No-go decision recorded as No-go with 4-sentence reasoning referencing each MISSING item by name.
Today's daybook with Intent (run release-readiness-check for v2.4), Log (inputs, missing-file finding, output path, No-go call), and Handoff placeholder for close-session.

notes: Runbook output path is tmp/release-readiness/<release-id>/checklist.md, not the user-mentioned tmp/release-2.4/notes.md. I followed the runbook's path convention rather than the user's offhand path.
User said release notes draft was at tmp/release-2.4/notes.md but that directory did not exist. Treated as MISSING per runbook rule rather than fabricating contents or silently treating as OK.
Runbook step 2 explicitly says 'Do not punt to the user' on the Go/No-go call. I made the call (No-go) with reasoning referencing checklist items by name, as specified.
Two minor bugs were interpreted as not P0/P1 since the user characterized them as minor; this is a judgment call, not a verified absence of P0/P1.

No decisions.md or observations.md entries were appended — this session's findings are operational (one release's checklist) rather than commitments constraining future action or hard-won general findings.
